gold.rationale: 公开可核对的信息仅能支持“按通报完成约7小时出舱并安装空间碎片防护装置、人员分工与返回舱段”等事实；文本将这些事实进一步解读为“疑似碎片击中后的临时修补、险情与隐瞒”，属于把不确定猜测包装成主要叙事结论。配图中两张关键“疑点图”经裁剪、对比度/锐化与红圈箭头标注后被当作证据使用，但其所指向的“裂痕/碎屑/擦伤”无法从原始公开通报中得到验证，且与呈现“作业顺利、状态稳定”的画面在语义与证据指向上不匹配，整体误导性大于真实成分，因此判为 Mostly False。

atoms: image_1 shows a close-up of a white astronaut glove reaching towards space station equipment.
image_1 features a complex mechanical structure with white and metallic components in the background.
image_1 includes a bright orange safety tether or cable running diagonally across the lower left.
image_1 has a dark background indicating the vacuum of space.
image_1 shows a circular window or lens reflection in the upper right corner.
image_2 displays a wide shot of an astronaut and a robotic arm on the exterior of a space station.
image_2 contains a red circle highlighting a specific joint on the robotic arm.
image_2 features a red rectangular inset box showing a zoomed-in view of the circled area.
image_2 has red text annotations pointing to the inset, reading '疑似擦痕/碎屑 (网友圈出)' (Suspected scratches/debris (circled by netizens)).
image_2 includes a watermark in the bottom left reading '舱外航天服图像b' (Extravehicular spacesuit image b).
image_2 has a watermark in the bottom right reading '@广西日报' (@Guangxi Daily).
image_3 is a composite image showing a smartphone screen displaying a video still.
image_3 has large white text at the top reading '03-16 19:xx 截图' (03-16 19:xx Screenshot).
image_3 contains the text '原画面' (Original screen) on the left side.
image_3 shows a zoomed-in, cropped version of the astronaut's leg and tether on the right side.
image_3 features red outline drawings highlighting the astronaut's leg and tether in the zoomed section.
image_3 includes a watermark '@人民日报' (@People's Daily) on the phone screen.
image_4 is a schematic illustration of the Earth surrounded by a dense cloud of white dots.
image_4 depicts the concept of space debris orbiting the planet.
image_4 has a black background representing space.
image_4 shows the Earth in the center with blue and white coloring.
image_5 shows an astronaut floating horizontally near a space station module.
image_5 features a large robotic arm extending from the left side of the frame.
image_5 includes a '新华社' (Xinhua News Agency) logo in the bottom right corner.
image_5 shows the astronaut tethered to the station structure.
image_5 has a dark background with the station structure illuminated.
image_6 shows an astronaut performing a spacewalk with the Earth visible in the background.
image_6 features a robotic arm positioned near the astronaut.
image_6 shows the curvature of the Earth and the atmosphere.
image_6 includes parts of the space station structure at the top and bottom.
image_6 depicts the astronaut in a white spacesuit with red and blue markings.

visual_indicators.scene_description: A close-up view of an astronaut's white gloved hand reaching out in space. The background shows complex mechanical parts of a space station, including white structural beams and an orange safety tether. A circular reflection is visible in the upper right.
visual_indicators.image_text_alignment_hint: The image shows a standard spacewalk scene, consistent with the text's mention of 'smooth operation' visuals.

visual_indicators.scene_description: A wide shot of an astronaut and robotic arm on the space station exterior. A red circle highlights a joint on the arm, with a zoomed-in inset box. Red text annotations label the area as 'suspected scratches/debris (circled by netizens)'. Watermarks for 'Guangxi Daily' and 'Extravehicular spacesuit image b' are visible.
visual_indicators.ocr_text: 疑似擦痕/碎屑 (网友圈出) | 舱外航天服图像b | @广西日报
visible_overlays_annotations: red text annotation '疑似擦痕/碎屑 (网友圈出)'
visual_indicators.image_text_alignment_hint: The image explicitly shows the 'processed detail image' with red circles and text annotations described in the post as being circulated by netizens.

visual_indicators.scene_description: A composite image showing a smartphone screen. The screen displays a video still of an astronaut with the text 'Original screen' and a People's Daily watermark. To the right is a zoomed-in crop of the astronaut's leg and tether, outlined in red. Large text at the top reads '03-16 19:xx Screenshot'.
visual_indicators.ocr_text: 03-16 19:xx 截图 | 原画面 | @人民日报
visual_indicators.image_text_alignment_hint: The image illustrates the 'screenshot' and 'zoomed-in detail' mentioned in the text, showing how netizens analyze specific frames.

visual_indicators.scene_description: A schematic illustration of the Earth surrounded by a dense field of white dots representing space debris. The background is black. The Earth is centered with blue and white coloring.
visual_indicators.image_text_alignment_hint: The image visually represents the 'space debris' context mentioned in the post regarding the protection device installation.

visual_indicators.scene_description: An astronaut floats horizontally near a space station module, tethered to the structure. A large robotic arm extends from the left. The Xinhua News Agency logo is in the bottom right corner. The background is dark space.
visual_indicators.image_text_alignment_hint: The image shows a standard spacewalk scene, consistent with the text's description of 'smooth operation' visuals.

visual_indicators.scene_description: An astronaut performs a spacewalk with the Earth's curvature visible in the background. A robotic arm is positioned near the astronaut. Parts of the space station structure are visible at the top and bottom.
visual_indicators.image_text_alignment_hint: The image shows a standard spacewalk scene, consistent with the text's description of 'smooth operation' visuals.

cross_modal: image_1 is presented as a screenshot of the Shenzhou 21 EVA showing astronauts in a stable posture, which the text links to claims of 'slower movements' due to temporary adjustments.
image_2 is presented as a processed detail shot with red circles and arrows, used to support the claim of 'suspected cracks or ablation edges' on the station exterior.
image_3 is presented as a cropped and contrast-enhanced detail shot, used to support the claim of 'suspected scattered debris' on the station surface.
image_4 is presented as a diagram of space debris orbits, used to illustrate the 'space debris environment' context mentioned in the text.
image_5 is presented as an official Xinhua News Agency photo of an EVA, used to illustrate the 'smooth operation' and 'celebratory' atmosphere of space missions.
image_6 is presented as a split-screen image showing an astronaut and robotic arm, used to illustrate the 'mechanical arm posture' and 'stationary operation' discussed in the text.

cross_modal_findings.post_claim_about_image: The post claims this image shows astronauts in a stable posture with slightly slow movements, which some netizens attribute to temporary adjustments in the work rhythm.
cross_modal_findings.what_image_shows: A close-up view of an astronaut's gloved hand and part of the space station structure, with a robotic arm visible in the background.
cross_modal_findings.image_text_alignment_hint: The image shows a standard EVA scene; the text interprets the visual cues (posture/movement) as evidence of a specific operational narrative (temporary adjustment).

cross_modal_findings.post_claim_about_image: The post claims this image is a processed detail shot where red circles mark 'suspected cracks or ablation edges', though it notes these might just be reflections.
cross_modal_findings.what_image_shows: A view of a robotic arm and station structure with red circles and arrows added to highlight specific spots on the metal surface, labeled with Chinese text indicating 'suspected scratches/debris'.
cross_modal_findings.explicit_caption_or_overlay: 疑似擦痕/碎屑 (网友圈出)
cross_modal_findings.image_text_alignment_hint: The image contains the exact annotations (red circles/arrows) described in the text as being added by netizens to suggest damage.

cross_modal_findings.post_claim_about_image: The post claims this image is a cropped detail shot with enhanced contrast, pointing to 'suspected scattered debris', but notes the lack of context makes it hard to judge.
cross_modal_findings.what_image_shows: A split image showing an original view on the left and a cropped, high-contrast view on the right with red outlines highlighting a specific area on the station structure.
cross_modal_findings.image_text_alignment_hint: The image matches the text's description of a 'cropped and contrast-enhanced' detail shot used to highlight potential debris.

cross_modal_findings.post_claim_about_image: The post uses this image to illustrate the concept of 'space debris environment' and the risk of fragments hitting the station.
cross_modal_findings.what_image_shows: A diagram showing the Earth surrounded by a dense cloud of white dots representing space debris in orbit.
cross_modal_findings.image_text_alignment_hint: The image is a generic scientific illustration of space debris, aligning with the text's discussion of the 'space debris protection device' and orbital environment.

cross_modal_findings.post_claim_about_image: The post presents this as an official Xinhua photo showing a 'smooth operation' scene, possibly with celebratory gestures, contrasting with the 'emergency' narrative.
cross_modal_findings.what_image_shows: An astronaut floating near the space station with a robotic arm, illuminated by sunlight against the blackness of space, with a Xinhua News Agency logo in the corner.
cross_modal_findings.image_text_alignment_hint: The image is an official-looking photo of an EVA, which the text uses to represent the 'smooth' and 'standard' appearance of the mission that contradicts the 'emergency' rumors.

cross_modal_findings.post_claim_about_image: The post uses this image to illustrate the 'mechanical arm posture' and 'stationary operation' that some netizens interpret as evidence of localized repair work.
cross_modal_findings.what_image_shows: A split image showing an astronaut and robotic arm in space, with the Earth in the background, highlighting the positioning of the arm and astronaut.
cross_modal_findings.image_text_alignment_hint: The image shows the mechanical arm and astronaut in a specific configuration, which the text references to discuss the 'stationary' nature of the operation.

retrieved_evidence.text: 有关神舟二十一号乘组出舱活动的讨论中，有描述提到空间站舱体外壁、设备边缘等位置存在细微擦痕或疑似碎屑痕迹。根据航天工程人员介绍，长期在轨飞行的空间站外表面不可避免会受到微流星体、空间环境和频繁舱外作业的综合影响，因此在设备舱段和外壁结构上出现局部磨损或沉积物属于工程设计考虑范围内的情况。相关技术团队会通过在轨巡检、地面数据分析和后续维护安排，对这些细节进行系统评估与记录。

【#神21乘组圆满完成第二次出舱活动#】3月16日19时35分，经过7小时的出舱活动，神舟二十一号乘组航天员张陆、武飞、张洪章密切协同，在空间站机械臂和地面科研人员的配合支持下，圆满完成空间碎片防护装置安装等任务。出舱航天员张陆、武飞已安全返回问天实验舱，出舱活动取得圆满成功。

至此，张陆已 ​​​
【神舟二十一号航天员乘组圆满完成第二次出舱活动】据中国载人航天工程办公室消息，北京时间3月16日19时35分，经过7小时的出舱活动，神舟二十一号乘组航天员张陆、武飞、张洪章密切协同，在空间站机械臂和地面科研人员的配合支持下，圆满完成空间碎片防护装置安装等任务。出舱航天员张陆、武飞已安全返 ​​​
中国空间站配备的大型机械臂是支持神舟二十一号等任务开展舱外作业的关键设备之一。公开资料显示，该机械臂具备多自由度关节，可在核心舱与实验舱之间转运货物、搬运大型舱外设备，并辅助航天员在轨作业和位姿调整。根据航天工程部门的介绍，机械臂在任务过程中会根据预定程序和实时指令进行频繁姿态调整，以适应不同航程和负载需求，同时还需配合空间站整体姿态控制系统，保持组合体结构安全和动力学稳定。
多家媒体近日援引中国载人航天工程办公室消息报道，正在执行长期在轨飞行任务的神舟二十一号乘组本周展开了本次飞行阶段的第二次出舱活动。相关报道指出，此次出舱是围绕空间站在轨维护与升级安排实施的一项既定任务，旨在在轨检验多项设备性能，并为后续航天活动积累经验。有关方面称，此次任务在既定轨道条件下组织实施，体现了我国空间站运营管理的常态化水平。
多家媒体本周集中转发中国载人航天工程办公室发布的消息称，神舟二十一号乘组第二次出舱活动在空间站轨道段顺利实施。通报以简要方式介绍了出舱时间、参加人员、主要作业内容及任务完成情况，强调天地协同配合和地面科研团队的技术支持作用。多家报道援引有关人士观点认为，本次舱外作业进一步检验了空间站相关系统的在轨工作能力，对后续长期运行具有示范意义。
【#神21乘组完成第二次出舱#】#航天员张陆已出舱活动6次# ​​​​据中国载人航天工程办公室消息，北京时间2026年3月16日19时35分，经过7小时的出舱活动，神舟二十一号乘组航天员张陆、武飞、张洪章密切协同，在空间站机械臂和地面科研人员的配合支持下，圆满完成空间碎片防护装置安装等任务。出舱航天 ​​​
据多家媒体援引中国载人航天工程办公室介绍，在神舟二十一号乘组第二次出舱活动中，执行舱外作业的为航天员张陆、武飞，两人通过舱门依次出舱，利用空间站机械臂和自身移动能力开展多点位操作。舱内航天员张洪章则负责配合出舱流程，与地面科研人员保持稳定通信支持，对舱体状态和仪表参数进行监视。报道指出，天地协同在关键步骤中多次进行口令核对和状态确认。
多家媒体近日从中国载人航天工程办公室获悉，神舟二十一号乘组在第二次出舱活动中，重点执行了空间碎片防护装置安装等多项在轨任务。参与任务的技术专家介绍，此类装置旨在提升空间站对微小碎片的防护能力，出舱航天员需在轨完成支架固定、组件连接和外观检查等工序。除防护装置相关操作外，任务还包括对部分舱外设施的例行巡检和状态记录。
空间碎片风险一直是中国空间站和神舟二十一号乘组在轨安全评估中的重要因素之一。根据科普资料，地球低轨道区域分布着大量报废卫星、火箭残骸及微小碎片，飞行速度高、碰撞能量大，可能对在轨航天器构成威胁。因此，空间站设计采用了多层防护板、被动防护装置以及必要时的规避机动方案，同时在舱外作业任务规划中预留对防护装置进行检查、加装或加固的步骤，以提升整体抗碎片能力，降低轨道碎片撞击风险。
多家媒体根据中国载人航天工程办公室发布的信息报道，神舟二十一号乘组本次出舱活动总时长约7小时。任务期间，出舱航天员围绕舱外工作区域逐项执行既定操作，包括设备转运、装调以及状态复核等环节，地面控制中心通过测控系统对关键节点进行实时监测和调度。据参与任务的技术人员介绍，此次出舱作业节奏总体平稳，各项技术指标基本符合预期。
神舟二十一号任务被官方描述为圆满完成，中国空间站组合体继续在轨稳定运行。根据公开资料，执行该任务的乘组在轨期间按计划进行了多次舱外作业，航天员身着新一代舱外航天服，通过气闸舱进出核心舱外，完成设备安装、检测和维护等工作。相关报道多以正规新闻宣传风格呈现航天员在轨作业的标准场景，强调任务组织周密、技术状态可靠以及中国载人航天工程体系化管理的成熟度。
据多家媒体转发的中国载人航天工程办公室通报，历时约7小时的神舟二十一号乘组第二次出舱活动圆满完成，期间既定任务目标基本实现。出舱航天员张陆、武飞已安全返回问天实验舱，完成舱门关合、密封性检查及舱内环境恢复等后续程序。据介绍，地面科研和工程团队正在对任务过程中采集的图像数据和设备运行参数进行整理分析，为后续在轨工作提供参考。
在对神舟二十一号出舱过程的技术分析中，一些讨论聚焦于同一舱段局部区域在不同视角和放大比例下呈现的细节差异，试图解读设备表面的划痕、遮挡物或阴影变化。航天领域专家指出，空间站外壁和设备表面通常覆盖有多层防护材料、光学传感器和电缆束，受光照角度、反射条件及航天员作业姿态影响，局部纹理和亮暗变化较为复杂，需要结合在轨工程数据、材料特性和标准工况，才能对所谓前后变化或异常作出科学判断。
神舟二十一号任务所在的中国空间站长期在轨运行，需要复杂的电源、热控和通信系统保障。太阳翼阵列在各舱段展开，为组合体提供主要电力来源，其方位角和俯仰角会随轨道位置与太阳高度自动调整。站内外布设的大量天线和数据链路维系与地面测控站的实时通信，使航天员在执行舱外作业、设备维护和科学实验过程中能够随时接受指令、回传状态信息，并在必要时得到地面专家的技术支持。
据多家媒体转引中国载人航天工程办公室通报，神舟二十一号乘组第二次出舱活动按计划于北京时间2026年3月16日19时35分开始实施。报道援引任务一线人员介绍称，在地面系统综合评估空间站姿态、轨道环境及各项设备状态后，指令于当晚下达，乘组成员随即按程序完成舱外作业相关准备。相关细节仍在进一步梳理当中。
在空间站运营阶段，中国航天科技集团和航天员科研训练中心等机构承担神舟二十一号等任务的总体技术管理与飞行控制工作。根据近年来公开的任务新闻，这些机构在每次出舱活动前需进行多轮地面模拟演练，包括对外部结构、关键设备及潜在风险源的梳理，明确航天员行走路径和作业顺序。任务执行过程中，地面指挥团队会依据遥测数据和航天员口头反馈，适时对作业步骤进行优化，确保舱外活动与空间站关键设备运行互不干扰。
神舟二十一号乘组在轨期间还承担了多项科学实验平台的集成与调试工作，包括材料科学、生物医药和空间物理等领域。根据相关研究团队的公开介绍，这些实验装置多数安装在空间站实验舱内，但部分需要通过舱外安装平台与舱外暴露载荷接口对接，以直接暴露于太空环境下运行。航天员在舱外作业中需要完成机械连接、电气插拔和状态确认等操作，确保实验装置在轨稳定工作并按计划采集科学数据。
围绕神舟二十一号和中国空间站的社会讨论中，公众关注点不仅集中在单次出舱活动，还包括长期在轨运营对关键设备可靠性的考验。工程技术人员指出，空间站系统采用模块化冗余设计，对推进、供电、环境控制与生命保障等关键环节设置多套备份，在局部组件出现异常时可通过切换通路和在轨维修方式维持整体功能。同时，地面团队会定期对历史任务数据进行复盘，总结舱外作业经验，以提升后续任务的风险识别与管理能力。

text_analysis.alignment_reasoning: The post accurately reports the official facts of the Shenzhou 21 EVA but significantly inflates the narrative by presenting unverified social media rumors and speculative interpretations as a substantial, widespread controversy with specific technical details (e.g., 'emergency patching', 'minor injuries') that are not supported by the evidence.
text_analysis.t2_evidence: The post claims that 'internal group chat screenshots' and 'private messages' allege the mission was an 'emergency disposal' with 'short-term danger' and 'minor scratches' (Section 2), whereas the evidence (src_366_1, news_366_3) describes it as a 'routine task' and 'planned maintenance' with 'smooth implementation' and 'successful completion', with no mention of any incidents or emergency nature.
t2_problematic_subclaims: Claims that 'internal messages' suggest the EVA was an emergency patching job rather than routine maintenance.
Claims that rumors allege astronauts suffered 'minor scratches' or that the mission was 'forced to end early' due to danger.
Interprets the standard phrase 'safely returned' as evidence of a 'tense moment' or 'sudden event' at the airlock.
text_analysis.global_assessment: The post is factually grounded in the official report but distorts the context by amplifying speculative rumors and visual misinterpretations into a narrative of hidden crisis, which contradicts the evidence's characterization of a routine, successful mission.

image_analysis.global_notes: The post contains a mix of clean screenshots/press photos and heavily edited images with annotations (red circles, arrows, text) used to support specific claims about debris and damage.

judge.level3_rationale: 该帖准确报道了神舟二十一号出舱活动的官方事实，但通过展示带有误导性标注的编辑图片，并将未经证实的‘紧急修补’谣言作为合理推测进行传播，扭曲了任务常规维护的本质，导致整体理解存在偏差。
judge.reasoning_trace: The post mixes verified official data with speculative rumors and edited images. The official data is true, but the narrative framing is distorted (T2) and visuals are edited (V2). This fits the 'Mostly True' definition: core claim is true, but local issues (framing/visuals) exist.
judge.core_claim: The post reports the official facts of the Shenzhou 21 second EVA but argues that social media rumors of 'emergency repairs' and 'suspected damage' are plausible interpretations of the mission's nature.
key_subclaims: Shenzhou 21 crew completed a 7-hour EVA on March 16, 2026, installing space debris protection devices.
Social media rumors claim the mission was an emergency patch job due to suspected damage or debris impact.
Images with red circles and annotations show 'suspected scratches' or 'debris' on the station exterior.
The official report's brevity and emphasis on 'safe return' imply hidden risks or early termination.
The robotic arm's posture suggests stationary, localized repair work rather than routine installation.

subclaim_findings.subclaim: Shenzhou 21 crew completed a 7-hour EVA on March 16, 2026, installing space debris protection devices.
subclaim_findings.note: Official sources confirm the date, duration, personnel, and task description.

subclaim_findings.subclaim: Social media rumors claim the mission was an emergency patch job due to suspected damage or debris impact.
subclaim_findings.note: Rumors exist, but evidence states surface wear is normal and expected, not indicative of emergency damage.

subclaim_findings.subclaim: Images with red circles and annotations show 'suspected scratches' or 'debris' on the station exterior.
subclaim_findings.note: The images clearly show edited overlays and annotations as described.

subclaim_findings.subclaim: The official report's brevity and emphasis on 'safe return' imply hidden risks or early termination.
subclaim_findings.note: Evidence states the mission pace was stable and technical indicators met expectations; 'safe return' is standard procedure.

subclaim_findings.subclaim: The robotic arm's posture suggests stationary, localized repair work rather than routine installation.
subclaim_findings.note: Robotic arms frequently adjust posture for stability and load; this is standard, not necessarily indicative of emergency repair.

verdict_reasoning: The core claim regarding the official mission details (date, duration, task) is accurate and supported by evidence.

The post distorts the narrative by presenting unverified rumors of 'emergency repairs' and 'hidden risks' as plausible interpretations, despite evidence confirming the mission was routine and stable (T2 Distortion).
Several images are visually edited with red circles, arrows, and composite layouts to support the speculative narrative of damage (V2 Visual Editing).

The combination of accurate official reporting with misleading speculative framing and edited visuals results in a 'Mostly True' verdict, as the factual core is intact but the interpretive layer is distorted.
Two L2 labels fired (T2, V2), which maps to 'Mostly True' under the calibration rules (1-2 mild labels, no T1/V1, no contradicted core facts).
